skill.name: gpt-image-edit
skill.description: Guides image edits with OpenAI GPT Image 2 through the RunComfy CLI, including prompt patterns for preservation, text replacement, layout changes, and multi-reference edits.
skill.summary: Edit images with GPT Image 2 on RunComfy using focused prompts for preservation, text, layout, and reference images.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns, but most are Markdown examples, service URLs, placeholders, or prose. Confirmed risks are disclosed RunComfy CLI execution, third-party API submission of prompts and image URLs, output downloads, and local token storage. No evidence found of prompt injection, malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or a real weak cryptographic implementation.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Model API Receives User Content
medium_findings.description: The skill documents that the RunComfy CLI sends prompts and image references to the RunComfy model API, then downloads generated outputs. This is a legitimate disclosed workflow, but prompts, image URLs, and generated content leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation directly states that the CLI POSTs to the RunComfy model API and downloads RunComfy-hosted outputs. The risk is confirmed, but it is disclosed and aligned with the skill purpose.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to install, authenticate, and run the RunComfy CLI. This is expected for the skill, but marketplace users should understand that successful use depends on executing an external command-line tool.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command usage is explicit and repeated in examples. I found no evidence that user-controlled prompt text is shell-expanded by the skill file itself.

medium_findings.title: Local API Token Storage
medium_findings.description: The skill documents that RunComfy login stores an API token under the user configuration directory with owner-only file permissions. This is normal CLI behavior, but the stored token remains sensitive local credential material.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and mode are clearly documented. The stated 0600 mode reduces exposure, so this is a moderate operational risk rather than evidence of theft.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Service URLs Are Disclosed Dependencies
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to RunComfy homepage, model pages, documentation, and the declared model API endpoint. These are expected service dependencies for this skill, not covert callbacks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible in documentation and match the advertised RunComfy workflow. No evidence found of unrelated telemetry, callbacks, or suspicious domains.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal and Weak Crypto Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts correspond to Markdown relative links, URL placeholders, and examples, while the weak-crypto alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code. No executable cryptographic operation or local path traversal behavior was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose, Markdown links, placeholders, or headings rather than code. I found no evidence of weak hashing, encryption, or path traversal implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented External Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill centers on running `runcomfy run openai/gpt-image-2/edit`. This is an intentional integration pattern and should be treated as an external-command capability.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly documented and necessary for the skill. It is potentially dangerous only because it depends on a third-party CLI and remote service.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Content Transfer Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires publicly fetchable image URLs and submits request bodies to a hosted model API. Users should avoid private or sensitive image URLs unless they accept RunComfy processing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network transfer behavior is stated in the skill. It is a real privacy boundary, but not evidence of hidden exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Precise AI image edits often fail when prompts omit preservation rules or layout details. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured RunComfy prompts for GPT Image 2 edits.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use GPT Image 2 edit instead of related image models.
Provides RunComfy CLI examples for single-image and multi-reference edits.
Documents the required prompt, images, and size fields for the edit endpoint.
Gives prompting patterns for preservation, spatial edits, and multilingual text replacement.
Describes exit codes, retry cases, token handling, and outbound endpoint behavior.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI, a RunComfy account, and network access.
Uses publicly fetchable HTTPS image URLs, not private local image paths by default.
Supports up to 10 reference images for the documented endpoint.
Complex multi-step edits may drift and should be split into separate passes.

use_cases.title: Localize campaign artwork
use_cases.description: Replace in-image headlines or labels with translated text while preserving layout, product, and brand marks.

use_cases.title: Create controlled product variants
use_cases.description: Change backgrounds, shadows, or framing while keeping the main product identity stable.

use_cases.title: Compose reference-based visuals
use_cases.description: Combine a subject, scene, and lighting direction from numbered reference images in one edit request.

prompt_templates.title: Basic background replacement
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner single-image edit with preservation language.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GPT Image 2 edit through RunComfy. Keep the subject, pose, face, product details, and framing unchanged. Replace only the background with [new background]. Use size auto.

prompt_templates.title: Translate in-image headline
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate localization edit for a poster or ad.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GPT Image 2 edit. Keep the photograph, layout, lighting, and brand mark unchanged. Replace only the headline with "[exact text]" in [script or language], matching the original position and weight.

prompt_templates.title: Move a layout element
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced spatial edit for design layout control.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GPT Image 2 edit. Keep all people, products, colors, and brand marks unchanged. Move [element] from [current position] to [new position]. Do not change any other visible content.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-reference composition
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced edit using several image URLs as numbered references.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GPT Image 2 edit with numbered references. Take the subject from image 1, the setting from image 2, and the lighting style from image 3. Preserve subject identity and match the final composition naturally.

output_examples.input: A product photo needs a cleaner studio background without changing the item.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a RunComfy GPT Image 2 edit request that preserves product details and replaces only the background.

output_examples.input: A poster headline must be changed from English to Japanese while keeping the same design.
output_examples.output: The assistant writes a prompt that quotes the exact Japanese text and asks the model to preserve layout, font weight, and brand elements.

output_examples.input: A campaign image should place a person from one reference into a room from another reference.
output_examples.output: The assistant creates a numbered multi-reference prompt that assigns subject, scene, lighting, and preservation roles.

best_practices: Start prompts with what must remain unchanged before describing the edit.
Quote exact replacement text and identify the script or language when editing text.
Use size auto unless the edit intentionally changes the image framing.
anti_patterns: Do not combine many unrelated edits in one request when consistency matters.
Do not use sensitive private image URLs unless RunComfy processing is acceptable.
Do not paraphrase text that must appear exactly in the edited image.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an assistant prepare GPT Image 2 edit requests for the RunComfy CLI using tested prompt structures.

faq.question: Does it run locally only?
faq.answer: No. The workflow uses the local RunComfy CLI and a hosted RunComfy model API.

faq.question: Can it edit local image files directly?
faq.answer: The documented endpoint expects publicly fetchable HTTPS image URLs, so local files need suitable hosting first.

faq.question: How many reference images are supported?
faq.answer: The skill documents support for up to 10 image URLs, with the first image treated as primary.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with confidential images?
faq.answer: Use caution. Prompts and image URLs are sent to RunComfy, so confidential content needs an approved data handling path.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
